articles.title: Lebanon Parliament speaker says framework agreement 'will not pass'
articles.summary: Lebanon Parliament speaker says framework agreement 'will not pass' Lebanese Parliament Speaker and Hezbollah ally Nabih Berri says that the trilateral framework agreement between Lebanon, Israel and the United States will not pass as it doesn’t guarantee Lebanon’s rights. “This agreement will not pass, and it will not be implemented in its current form,” Berri says in a statement shared by his party, the Amal movement, adding that it was “an agreement of dictates, not an agreement that preserves Lebanon’s rights.” The deal paves the way for Lebanese-Israeli peace and conditions Israeli withdr

articles.title: Report says Israel rebrands Gaza displacement plan as 'Freedom of Movement'
articles.summary: Report says Israel rebrands Gaza displacement plan as 'Freedom of Movement' Israel has renamed its controversial plan to encourage Palestinians to leave Gaza, dropping the term "voluntary migration" in favour of "Freedom of Movement Plan", Aljazeera reported, citing Israel's Channel 13. The report said Israeli security officials and the Mossad have been instructed to stop using the “voluntary migration”, which had drawn sharp international criticism.  Channel 13 said Israeli officials believe the new name could make foreign governments more willing to cooperate with the plan. articles.summary: Gaza journalists work from tents after Israel destroys media offices Between 60 and 75 percent of journalists in Gaza have lost their homes or been forcibly displaced since October 7, 2023, according to the Freedoms Committee of the Palestinian Journalists Syndicate. In a report titled “Media without walls”, the committee said about 265 journalists have been killed since the start of Israel’s war on Gaza, making it one of the deadliest conflicts for media workers anywhere in the world. articles.summary: US and Iran agree to resume Strait of Hormuz talks: Report Iran and the United States have agreed to pause the latest fighting in the Gulf and return to talks over the Strait of Hormuz, Axios reports. The move could halt days of attacks and counterattacks that have pushed an interim peace agreement close to collapse. Axios, citing a senior US official, said the two sides are expected to meet in Qatar on Tuesday. articles.title: Gaza dialysis patients at risk as machines fail
articles.summary: Gaza dialysis patients at risk as machines fail Nearly half of the dialysis machines at Gaza’s largest hospital have stopped working as Israel’s blockade and repeated attacks continue to cripple the enclave’s health system. Dr Ghazi al-Yaziji, head of the artificial kidney department at al-Shifa Medical Complex, said a severe shortage of sodium bicarbonate, a substance needed to control dangerous acidity in the blood of kidney failure patients, has forced 25 machines out of service. articles.title: Israeli forces push deeper into southern Syria
articles.summary: Israeli forces push deeper into southern Syria Israeli forces have expanded their occupation in Syria’s southern Deraa province, setting up new checkpoints and moving military vehicles deeper into the area, Syria’s state news agency SANA reports. SANA said Israeli troops established a second checkpoint near the village of Sreyeh Jamlah in Deraa’s western countryside, about 200 metres from another checkpoint installed a day earlier. At least five Israeli military vehicles also advanced into the village of Abidin, where troops set up two checkpoints and searched people passing through the area.

articles.title: High Schools Emphasizing Personal Finance Education
articles.summary: 39 US states now require students to complete a personal finance course to graduate, up from just one state in 1998.
